notes: Verified via WebSearch 2026-07-11 (Wikipedia, US DOL, Britannica, HISTORY.com): first Labor Day observance September 5, 1882 in NYC (Central Labor Union march, ~10,000 workers, proposed by Matthew Maguire); became a federal holiday (first Monday of September) when President Grover Cleveland signed the law June 28, 1894, shortly after the Pullman Strike.

KEY DIFFERENTIATION FACT (addresses backlog's specific angle): the September date was a deliberate political choice, not coincidence — Cleveland favored it over May 1 to avoid linking the labor holiday to the 1886 Haymarket affair and strengthening socialist/anarchist movements. This 'why September not May' explanation is completely absent from the Arabic content reviewed.

Explicitly cross-linked and distinguished from the site's existing global labor-day event (May 1) — relatedSlugs includes 'labor-day' and FAQ directly answers 'لماذا يختلف الموعد' per the backlog's exact instruction.
Floating type (month 9, weekday 1/Monday, nth 1) — engine-native, no manual date calculation needed.
Global/diaspora event (_countryCode: us), aliasSlugs: [] — same pattern as thanksgiving-usa/christmas-usa/independence-day-usa.
